verdict_reasoning: SRC (proto-oncogene tyrosine kinase Src) is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase. It is anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, placing it on the cytoplasmic face. Its kinase domain, SH2, and SH3 domains are entirely intracellular. No portion of SRC reaches the extracellular face of the plasma membrane. It is therefore inaccessible to extracellular binders such as antibodies or ADCs targeting the protein body.
